stories.headline: Ukraine calls for air defences as Russia threatens more strikes on Kyiv after overnight attack
stories.summary: After an overnight assault that killed at least 20 people in Ukraine, the Kremlin stated it will increase the pace of its attacks to achieve its goals.

stories.headline: Gaza patients face agonising delays for medical evacuation

stories.headline: German prosecutors allege Ukraine ordered Nord Stream pipeline attack
stories.summary: Former Ukrainian soldier accused of 'war crime' and leading a team of divers to blow up Nord Stream pipelines.

stories.headline: China says Beijing plane crash pilot wrote about suicide

stories.summary: The 66-year-old, who died in the crash, had anxiety and referenced "ending his life" in his diary.

stories.headline: South Africa and Ghana in diplomatic row over alleged killing of migrant
stories.summary: South Africa denies claims that a Ghanaian citizen was killed in anti-migrant protests in Cape Town.

stories.headline: Tech, Finance Titans Redraw Stablecoin Map in Threat to Circle
stories.summary: For years, the stablecoin race has been defined by a simple objective: build the dominant digital dollar. This week’s turbulence in Circle Internet Group Inc.’s shares suggests investors are beginning to price a different future — one in which the biggest rewards may accrue not to the companies issuing stablecoins, but to those controlling how they move.

stories.headline: JPMorgan Says Saylor’s Strategy Adds New Risk to Bitcoin Market
stories.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. warns that Michael Saylor’s financing overhaul at Strategy Inc. has shaken up the dynamics of the Bitcoin market by introducing the risk that one of the cryptocurrency’s biggest buyers could also become a seller, adding a new source of uncertainty for investors.

stories.summary: Authorities say rainy season getting deadlier, with Ghana reporting 13 dead and floods hitting Benin, Togo and NigeriaFloods in Côte d’Ivoire have killed 59 people since May, the communication minister told a cabinet meeting in Abidjan.There are fears the toll could further rise as rescue teams continue to search for victims during the rainy season, which runs from May until July, the minister, Amadou Coulibaly, added. stories.headline: Young Indonesian couple publicly caned after kissing on TikTok
stories.summary: Unmarried man and woman whipped 21 times each because they had violated province’s version of Islamic lawA young couple in Indonesia’s conservative Aceh province have been publicly caned after a Sharia court convicted them of violating Islamic law by kissing during a TikTok livestream.The court ordered the couple, a 22-year-old man and a 25-year-old woman, to be whipped with a rattan cane 21 times each for kissing without being married. stories.headline: US job growth slows in June; hospitality sheds roles despite World Cup
stories.summary: Labour force participation dropped to its lowest level since March 2021, reaching 61.5 percent in June.

stories.headline: UK’s Starmer apologises for the state’s role in decades of forced adoptions
stories.summary: UK government and Christian churches oversaw system that separated 185,000 babies from their mothers from 1949 to 1976.

stories.headline: Keir Starmer issues state apology for decades of forced adoptions practices in UK
stories.summary: Previously, the then-Conservative government declined to apologise, arguing that the state had not supported these practices. Starmer has now acknowledged the government's role in forced adoptions.

stories.headline: US job growth plummets as eurozone unemployment holds at record low
stories.summary: New labour market data highlights a growing transatlantic divide, with US payroll additions slowing drastically, while the Eurozone unemployment rate remains anchored at an all-time low.

stories.summary: The chief justice faced down the president, forged unlikely coalitions and achieved long-sought goals.

stories.summary: The endorsement is the first in a contested Senate primary by Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez this year, in a state that Democrats believe they must hold this fall to win a Senate majority.

stories.headline: Republican Senate Candidates Start Their Own Super PACs to Gain Cash Advantage
stories.summary: Most of the party’s top candidates are starting their own super PACs instead of relying on a powerful group run by Washington leaders. The move allows them to seize control of their financial destinies.

stories.headline: How Trump Made ‘Y.M.C.A.’ His Anthem, Despite the Village People and Victor Willis’s Mixed Feelings
stories.summary: On social media, he said the Village People’s lead singer, Victor Willis, was with him “right from the beginning.” But the president has a more complicated history with the group.

stories.headline: Controversial bishops ordained as Pope warns of 'schism' in Catholic Church

stories.headline: A plane crashed into a tower in Beijing but China is not saying what happened

stories.headline: Koeman resigns, while Netherlands report racist abuse
stories.summary: Ronald Koeman resigns as Netherlands manager after their World Cup exit, which led to "appalling" racist abuse aimed at players who missed penalties.

stories.summary: The blast was caused by an explosive device which appeared to contain bolts and pellets, the head of Monaco's government said.

stories.headline: Founder of Asian super-app Gojek sentenced to years in jail for corruption
